title: Chaman Mahal
tagline: Chaman Mahal — heritage city of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Chaman Mahal is a palace in the Bhopal district of Madhya Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Chaman Mahal is a palace in the Bhopal district of Madhya Pradesh, India. It was built by Afghan Commander Dost Mohammad Khan, who was the ruler of Islamnagar in 1715. It is located 11 km away from Bhopal, capital of Madhya Pradesh. 'Chaman' means garden and hence the palace is also known as Garden palace, having an attractive garden in the middle of palace with multiple fountains.

topPlaces.name: Islamnagar, Bhopal
topPlaces.description: Islamnagar (officially known as Jagdishpur since February 2023) is a panchayat village in the Bhopal district of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is located in the Huzur tehsil and the Phanda block.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chaman Mahal?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chaman Mahal is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chaman Mahal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Raja Bhoj Airport, Bhopal (11 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhopal Junction (10 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chaman Mahal?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chaman Mahal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chaman Mahal?
faq.a: Stays in Chaman Mahal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chaman Mahal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Islamnagar, Bhopal, Bherupura, Kanchbavli, Ghasipura, Bhopal, Kalyanpura, Bhopal.
